Title: Structural Basis of Cycloaddition in Biosynthesis of Iboga and Aspidosperma Alkaloids

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product scaffold from common cyclization substrate
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: angryline (3c), a stabilized form of dehydrosecodine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes cyclization of angryline/dehydrosecodine to form the iboga alkaloid (+)-catharanthine; also catalyzes the reverse retro-Mannich conversion of catharanthine to 16-carbomethoxycleaviminium under assay conditions
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key product-determining cyclization step; branch-selective scaffold-forming enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Heterologous in vitro enzyme assay / E. coli-expressed plant enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: IPTG-inducible heterologous bacterial expression system (vector pOPINF)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant-derived bioactive alkaloid; precursor/scaffold related to compounds with anti-cancer and anti-addiction activities
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays; optimal reaction conditions tested across pH; mutant assays commonly in 100 mM TRIS-HCl pH 8.5; 37 °C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzymatic assay with isolated angryline substrate; crystal structure determination (PDB 6RT8); mutational analysis; LC-MS/UPLC-MS product profiling; ligand cocrystallization
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Product selectivity altered by mutations; no plant-level tradeoff reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme engineering target for redesigning alkaloid scaffold selectivity and generating unnatural iboga/aspidosperma alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CS uses angryline/dehydrosecodine-derived substrate to produce (+)-catharanthine. 2. CS is a carboxylesterase homologue repurposed as a cyclase with lost ancestral hydrolytic activity. 3. Structural and mutational data indicate CS controls substrate conformation to favor iboga scaffold formation. 4. CS can also catalyze retro-Mannich opening of catharanthine to 16-carbomethoxycleaviminium, supporting a possible stepwise ionic mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Crystal structure contained bound 16-carbomethoxycleaviminium rather than catharanthine; catalytic triad is disrupted, including His replacement by Tyr; oxyanion hole altered from Gly-Gly to Ala-Gly.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product scaffold from common cyclization substrate
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: angryline (3c), a stabilized form of dehydrosecodine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes cyclization of angryline/dehydrosecodine to form the aspidosperma alkaloid (-)-tabersonine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key product-determining cyclization step; branch-selective scaffold-forming enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Heterologous in vitro enzyme assay / E. coli-expressed plant enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: IPTG-inducible heterologous bacterial expression system (vector pOPINF)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant-derived bioactive alkaloid scaffold in monoterpene indole alkaloid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays; pH dependence tested; mutant assays commonly in 100 mM TRIS-HCl pH 8.5; 37 °C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzymatic assay with isolated angryline substrate; crystal structure determination (PDB 6RS4); docking; mutational analysis; LC-MS/UPLC-MS product profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutations can redirect product profile toward catharanthine or abolish activity; no plant-level tradeoff reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for enzyme engineering to alter scaffold selectivity and generate non-natural alkaloid products
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TS converts angryline to (-)-tabersonine. 2. TS is a structurally repurposed carboxylesterase-like cyclase lacking hydrolytic activity. 3. Structural modeling suggests TS positions the methyl acrylate carbonyl in plane with the indole to favor aspidosperma scaffold formation. 4. TS did not catalyze detectable reverse reaction with tabersonine, implying mechanistic differences from CS.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Canonical catalytic His is replaced by Tyr297; Y297H failed to restore esterase activity; missing loop may expose substrate to bulk water and facilitate protonation relevant to cyclization mechanism.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Iboga alkaloid branch leading to coronaridine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms unstable immediate product that is reduced to target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes cyclization of angryline/dehydrosecodine-derived tautomer to form an unstable iminium intermediate en route to (-)-coronaridine; likely also promotes substrate tautomerization
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-selective scaffold- and stereochemistry-determining cyclization step
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Heterologous in vitro enzyme assay / E. coli-expressed plant enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: IPTG-inducible heterologous bacterial expression system (vector pOPINF)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant-derived iboga alkaloid scaffold related to anti-addiction bioactive compounds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays; CorS activity evaluated with DPAS coupling; D2O labeling supported tautomerization requirement; mutant assays at 37 °C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of (-)-coronaridine via unstable (-)-coronaridine iminium intermediate and altered dehydrosecodine tautomerization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coupled in vitro enzymatic assay with angryline and DPAS; crystal structure determination (PDB 6RJ8); docking; D2O labeling evidence from cited work; mutational analysis; LC-MS/UPLC-MS product profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutations broaden product spectrum to include tabersonine and catharanthine, indicating selectivity/plasticity tradeoff; no plant-level tradeoff reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important engineering target for controlling alkaloid enantioselectivity, tautomer-specific cyclization, and expansion of iboga alkaloid diversity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CorS works with DPAS to convert angryline-derived substrate into (-)-coronaridine. 2. CorS likely first forms unstable (-)-coronaridine iminium, which DPAS reduces to the final product. 3. CorS probably promotes tautomerization of dehydrosecodine in addition to cyclization, explaining enantioselective divergence from CS. 4. Structural and mutational data implicate helix alpha5 and active-site residues in controlling this unique cyclization mode.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Canonical catalytic His is replaced by Phe; docking required repositioning of Phe300; active site accommodates (-)-iboga scaffold; tautomerization to dehydrosecodine isomer 3e is proposed to underlie switch in enantioselectivity.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Common upstream reductive branch supplying cyclization substrate and downstream reduction for coronaridine branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream precursor-forming reduction step and downstream partner reduction step in coronaridine branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and reduces immediate cyclization intermediate in coronaridine formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: precondylocarpine acetate (1); and (-)-coronaridine iminium (9) in coupled assay
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: angryline/dehydrosecodine precursor pool from precondylocarpine acetate; (-)-coronaridine (6) from iminium intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces precondylocarpine acetate to generate unstable intermediate(s) leading to angryline/dehydrosecodine; in T. iboga coupled assays reduces coronaridine iminium to (-)-coronaridine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established in this paper; supporting precursor-supply and intermediate-reduction enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Heterologous in vitro enzyme assay / E. coli-expressed plant enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: CrDPAS: KU865331; T. iboga DPAS: NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Coupled in vitro assays with NADPH; 37 °C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides reduced substrate/intermediate required for cyclization pathway and completes reduction of coronaridine iminium to (-)-coronaridine in CorS assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coupled enzyme assays with cyclases; product isolation and LC-MS profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be paired with cyclases for pathway reconstruction and alkaloid scaffold engineering, but was not the main mechanistic focus of this paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DPAS converts precondylocarpine acetate to unstable intermediate(s) leading to angryline/dehydrosecodine. 2. In combination with CS or TS it supports formation of catharanthine or tabersonine in prior coupled assays. 3. In combination with CorS it reduces unstable coronaridine iminium to (-)-coronaridine.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although essential in coupled assays, the paper concludes the sole function of CS, TS and CorS is cyclization after angryline formation; DPAS is not the main structural focus of the study.
